data.title: Import prices rise 0.1%, export prices rise 0.5% in June
data.description:
U.S. import prices advanced 0.1 percent in June, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported today, following a 0.4-percent decrease in May. Higher prices for nonfuel imports more than offset lower prices for fuel imports in June.

Prices for U.S. imports fell 0.2 percent from June 2024 to June 2025, matching the 12- month decline for the year ended May 2025. Those were the largest annual decreases since the index fell 0.9 percent for the year ended February 2024.

Prices for U.S. exports increased 0.5 percent in June, after declining 0.6 percent the previous month. Prices for U.S. exports increased 0.5 percent in June following a decrease of 0.6 percent in May and an advance of 0.1 percent in April.

Higher prices for both nonagricultural and agricultural exports drove the June increase. U.S. export prices increased 2.8 percent from June 2024 to June 2025, the largest over-the- year rise since the index advanced 2.8 percent for the year ended January 2025.

Source

data.title: Roblox introduces new safety tools for users, parents
data.description:
Roblox (NYSE:RBLX) on Thursday launched a suite of new tools that will establish guardrails to protect young users and allow for safer communication on the virtual gaming platform.

The company has introduced trusted connections, which will let users aged 13 and older connect and chat more freely with people they know and trust, alongside a new age estimation technology that will help confirm users’ ages and unlock trusted connections.

It also introduced privacy and well-being tools for users to have more ways to manage notifications, screen time, and privacy, and a new insights tool for parents to check up on their children.

"Safety has always been foundational to everything we do at Roblox. Our goal is to lead the world in safety and civility for online gaming," the company's chief safety officer, Matt Kaufman, said in an official statement.

Source: Press Release

data.title: INVO Fertility announces a 1:3 reverse stock split
data.description:
• INVO Fertility (NASDAQ:IVF) will effect a 1-for-3 reverse split of its issued and outstanding and authorized common stock effective as of 12:01 a.m. Eastern Time on July 21, 2025.
  • Commencing with the opening of trading on The Nasdaq on July 21, 2025, the company’s common stock will trade on a post-split basis under the same trading symbol, “IVF”.
  • The reverse stock split is primarily intended to bring the company into compliance with Nasdaq’s $1.00 per share minimum bid price requirement for continued listing.
  • IVF -2.4% premarket to $0.8001.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock futures mixed as retail sales report comes in hot
data.description:
Stock index futures searched for direction on Thursday, as retail sales rose more than expected in June and investors assessed more earnings reports. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.2%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.48%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.92%.

June retail sales came in at +0.6% M/M to $720.1B vs. +0.1% consensus and -0.9% prior (unrevised), while core retail sales, excluding motor vehicles and parts, came in at +0.5% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and -0.6% prior (revised from -0.3%).

Initial jobless claims unexpectedly fell in the past week.

Stocks came off a volatile session on Wednesday, especially after reports of Trump's plans for Powell, but eventually ended in the green. Major averages also benefited from a softer-than-anticipated wholesale inflation report.

“For about an hour (on Wednesday) we had a brief glimpse of the likely market reaction as investors started to view Powell’s removal as a serious prospect. Notably, there was a huge steepening in the yield curve as investors ramped up the prospect of a near-term rate cut,” Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said. 

Trump said on Wednesday that it's “highly unlikely” he'll fire Powell “unless he has to leave for fraud.” Earlier reporting on the day indicated that the President would fire the Fed Chair soon.

“It feels like ancient history now, but before the Powell story, markets had seen some positive momentum after the PPI report was softer than expected. So that helped to ease fears about inflation, and investors viewed the release as enough to keep Fed rate cuts in play for this year,” Allen added.

 

“June import and export prices matter. Import prices are pre-trade tax. If they deviate from previous trends (and those trends might be deflation), that suggests exporters are adjusting to take some of the cost of the taxes. If they follow established trends, the burden of trade taxes falls entirely on the US part of the supply chain,” Donovan added. 

The Fed's balance sheet will land later in the day. 

Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) will release its quarterly results after the closing bell.

data.title: Jacobs selected to design light rail extension in Seattle
data.description:
• Jacobs (NYSE:J) said on Thursday that it has been selected to provide design services for Sound Transit's West Seattle Link Extension. 
  • This project will expand Seattle's light rail network, enhancing connectivity for residential and economic centers. 
  • The West Seattle Link Extension will add 4.1 miles of light rail, a new bridge, a tunnel, and four new stations, connecting Seattle's SODO neighborhood with West Seattle's Alaska Junction neighborhood. 
  • Jacobs will lead Phase 1 design and design validation for the extension.
  • Expected to begin service in 2032, the West Seattle Link Extension will reduce travel times on key lines by up to 50 percent. It will also offer commuters an alternative to the West Seattle Bridge during periods of peak demand.

data.title: EQT AB Non-GAAP EPS of €0.58, revenue of €1.27B
data.description:
• EQT AB (publ) press release (OTCPK:EQBBF): 1H Non-GAAP EPS of €0.578.

  • EBITDA amounted to €806m (€609m), corresponding to an EBITDA margin of 60% (56%). Fee-related EBITDA amounted to €615m (€568m), corresponding to a Fee-related EBITDA margin of 54% (54%).

data.title: Vitrolife AB (publ) reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Vitrolife AB (publ) press release (OTCPK:VTRLY): Q2  Net income was SEK 100 (143) million, resulting in earnings per share of SEK 0.74 (1.06).
  • Gross margin decreased to 58.0% (59.9) also negatively impacted by currency.
  • Earnings before depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) decreased to SEK 243 (327) million, resulting in an EBITDA margin of 27.8% (34.7), significantly impacted by negative currency effect.
  • Operating cash flow amounted to SEK 151 million (236).

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Abbott Laboratories Q2 beats revenue estimates; narrows FY outlook range
HP Enterprise rises after getting Overweight rating at J.P. Morgan
data.description:
J.P. Morgan has rated Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) Overweight from a Not Rated designation, with a December 2026 price target of $30 on the stock.

Shares of Hewlett Packard Enterprise rose about 2% premarket on Thursday.

Analysts led by Samik Chatterjee said they have moved to the Overweight rating following the completion of Juniper Networks' acquisition by HPE which enhances its position and firmly establishes it as one of the largest Networking companies, with one of the broadest portfolios encompassing switches, wireless access points, routers, and more.

The analysts added that with the increasing mix of higher margin networking revenues, supplemented by a plethora of cost synergies, they see a pathway for HPE to achieve earnings of at least $2.70 by fiscal 2027, underscoring the significant upside potential for the shares.

However, the analysts noted that recent concerns relative to execution is likely to lead investors to more steadily warm-up to the higher earnings multiple while watching critical milestones on integration.
